email.subject: TradeGravity Semiconductor Pulse · 2023-12
email.preview: Three cited USA-China semiconductor observations for 2023-12; monthly movement is kept separate from publication revisions.
email.markdown:
# TradeGravity Semiconductor Pulse

Observation window: 2023-12 vs 2023-11 · publication status: baseline

## Korea, Rep. selected chip trade increased

Selected monthly HS6 trade with USA and China moved from US$18.91B to US$19.21B (+1.6%).

Interpretation boundary: A change in the selected two-anchor customs observations worth investigating; it does not establish production, demand, or causality.

## Japan two-anchor balance shifted toward USA

China's share of the selected USA-plus-China total moved from 50.4% to 50.3% (-0.1 percentage points).

Interpretation boundary: The sign describes movement within the published USA-China anchor pair, not political alignment or global market share.

## Korea, Rep. · Processors and controllers increased

HS6 854231 selected trade moved from US$6.14B to US$6.24B (+1.6%).

Interpretation boundary: This is a product-level customs observation against USA and China, not company revenue, capacity, or a shipment route.

Review the cited evidence before forwarding or publishing this draft.

[Inspect the evidence]({{BASE_URL}}/?tab=semiconductors)

Data scope: selected monthly UN Comtrade HS6 observations against USA and China. Not investment, legal, or policy advice.
email.primary_goal: Return the reader to the cited TradeGravity evidence

slides.headline: Read the clocks separately
slides.body: Month-to-month customs movement and publish-to-publish revisions answer different questions. Neither proves causality or a physical route.

slides.headline: Inspect the evidence
slides.body: Open TradeGravity's Chip Lens for periods, values, sources, coverage, and limitations.

caveats: Monthly customs observations can be volatile, incomplete, and revised.
USA and China values are the two published anchor relationships, not world totals or physical shipment routes.
Publication-to-publication revisions are separate from economic month-to-month movement.
